behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is function-level formula evaluation logic (range expansion + numeric aggregation), not module handoff. No OS/network/subprocess and no AI/ML dependency. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is purely computational: "evaluates to the correct sum" for one formula.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-memory cell map with A1..A10 numeric values, evaluate `SUM(A1:A10)` via the formula evaluator function/object boundary, and assert exact numeric result including edge values (e.g., negatives, decimals).

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at module/state handoff: dependency resolution + multi-cell evaluation order across references. A single-function unit test can miss bugs in how referenced computed values propagate. No real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. System rejected because behavior does not require external infrastructure; text is in-engine cascade evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with A1 raw value, B1 formula `=A1*2`, C1 formula `=B1+1`; run full recalculation pipeline and assert C1 value. Then change A1 and assert cascade updates through B1 to C1.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a graph-level behavior across multiple cells and dependency tracking, not just one parser function. Requires interaction between reference resolver and evaluator/graph traversal. No OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. System rejected because no external infra is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build workbook state with A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1`, invoke recalculation on engine graph traversal path, assert explicit circular-reference error type/code and no unbounded evaluation loop.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic lookup algorithm semantics for one built-in function. No required cross-module I/O boundary, and no AI/ML dependency. Integration rejected because behavior text is function correctness: "VLOOKUP with an exact match returns... and returns an error when key is not found."
behaviors.test_description: Call VLOOKUP evaluator with in-memory table ranges for (a) present key exact-match and (b) missing key; assert returned cell value and specific not-found error.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: This is expression evaluation/branching logic within formula interpreter at local function/object boundary. No OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. Higher levels rejected because behavior is nested conditional semantics in a single expression.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF formulas against multiple A1 values (<=10, 11..20, >20) and assert exact output strings and branch selection.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic string assembly rule for one function, including empty-cell handling. No module-boundary infrastructure requirement, no AI/ML. Integration rejected because text specifies local function semantics only.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke CONCATENATE evaluation with in-memory arguments including empty cells and separators; assert resulting string has expected delimiters and omitted empties.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet reference resolution requires interaction between parser/name resolver/workbook model and evaluator; bug can occur at module handoff even if per-function math is correct. No real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. System rejected because behavior is in-memory workbook wiring only.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 formula `=Sheet2!A1+1` and Sheet2 A1 value; run engine resolution + evaluation path and assert computed result and correct sheet binding.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure mode is dependency graph orchestration and propagation across cells/modules after mutation, which is beyond isolated function logic. No OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. System rejected because no external infrastructure is needed by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Set up dependency DAG (including branching chain), mutate a source cell, trigger incremental/full recalc, assert final dependent values and verify evaluation order respects topological constraints (e.g., via instrumentation hooks or ordered trace).

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness depends on interaction between edit history/state snapshots and recalculation/dependency modules. A unit test of only history stack or only evaluator can miss cross-module restore bugs. No OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. System rejected because behavior is local engine state management.
behaviors.test_description: In an in-memory workbook, edit a formula that affects dependents, recalc, then invoke undo; assert restored formula text and all dependent cell values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets serialization/deserialization contract across persistence and workbook model modules with local filesystem round-trip. Real network/subprocess not involved; no AI/ML. System was considered due disk I/O, but rejected because behavior text is format fidelity ("saving... and loading... identical values/formulas") and can be reliably caught with local temp-file integration without OS-level event/process semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook fixture with formulas and floating-point edge values, save to a temp file using production binary serializer, reload via production loader, and assert equality of formula text, computed values, and float tolerances/bit-preserving expectations per spec.
